topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The box sat on the entryway table, a garish splash of pink and yellow against the muted beige of the hallway. I stared at it, my jaw tight enough to crack enamel. No note. Absolutely nothing. Just a cascade of overly cheerful lilies and some aggressively bright roses. I picked up a stem, the petals feeling slick and alien in my grip, and immediately dropped it back onto the wrapping paper with a sharp snap. Why? Who sends unsolicited bouquets without an explanation? I paced the length of the living room three times, my footsteps heavy and rhythmic on the hardwood floor. Every instinct screamed that this was some elaborate, passive-aggressive gesture, and I was furious at the sheer audacity of it. I needed to find the sender, needed to know the motive behind this saccharine display of unwelcome attention.

[story 2]

When the delivery person handed me the package, I didn't even look up from the spreadsheet I was reviewing. My knuckles were white where I gripped my coffee mug, steam momentarily fogging my glasses. Inside the box, nestled amongst the crinkled tissue paper, were flowers—a riot of deep reds and purples that seemed entirely out of place in my sterile workspace. I let out a low, controlled exhale, the sound vibrating slightly in my chest. I carefully lifted one bloom, examining the perfect curve of its shape as if it held some hidden message of insult. I set it down with deliberate, almost violent precision. I stood up, the chair scraping loudly against the floor, and began to stride towards the front door, my posture rigid, every muscle coiled tight with displeasure at the interruption.

[story 3]

I found the flowers abandoned on the kitchen counter, sitting next to the fruit bowl like a bizarre, unwelcome centerpiece. My breath hitched. I didn't move for a full minute, just observing the arrangement—a haphazard collection of sunflowers and daisies that felt utterly ridiculous. A deep, simmering heat rose in my neck, and I clenched my fists until my nails dug crescent moons into my palms. I finally snatched the bouquet, the stems cold against my fingers. I turned on the faucet, letting the cold water run over my hands, trying to shock the tension out of my system. "Seriously?" I muttered to the empty room, my voice low and dangerously flat. "No words? Just this?" The sheer lack of effort in the gesture made my temper flare, making my shoulders tense up to my ears.
